title: St. Louis Housing Market Report — March 2026
content:
St. Louis Housing Market Report — March 2026 (Source: Homes.com/CoStar Analytics)

HEADLINE: St. Louis home prices rise quickly as inventory grows and sales slow. Strong price growth alongside noticeable increase in homes for sale, while sales activity fell from last year's pace. Prices remained firmer than many markets nationally, supported by affordability.

SALE PRICES:
- Median home sale price: $285,000 (up 7.5% YoY, +$20,000)
- National median: $385,000 (up 1.3% YoY)
- St. Louis ranks 3rd nationally for annual price growth (behind Kansas City and San Francisco)
- Highest percentage gain in March since 2021
- 36th-highest-priced market among top 40 largest markets
- St. Louis median is 26% below national median
- Market peak: $299,000 (June 2024)

BY PROPERTY TYPE:
- Single-family median: $299,450 (up 9.9% YoY, +$26,950)
- Townhome median: down 3.7% YoY
- Condo median: down 16.3% YoY

INVENTORY:
- Active listings: 8,411 (up 14.1% YoY, +1,037 listings)
- National active listings up 10.7%
- Ranks 9th nationally for inventory growth
- Still 28.4% below March 2019 level of 15,655
- Months of supply: 2.9 (bottom third nationally, below pre-pandemic levels)
- Single-family active listings: 7,338 (up 13%)
- Townhome active listings: 326 (up 38.7%)
- Condo active listings: 747 (up 16%)

HOME SALES:
- Total sales: 2,469 (down 6.4% YoY, -169 sales)
- National home sales up 0.5%
- Ranks 36th among top 40 markets for annual sales change
- Third straight annual March decline
- Single-family sales: 2,220 (down 6.7%)
- Townhome sales: 83 (up 112.8% from low base)
- Condo sales: 166 (down 24.5%)

DAYS ON MARKET: Average 47 days (vs. 66 days nationally)

KEY TAKEAWAYS:
- St. Louis remains one of the most affordable major housing markets in the U.S.
- Strong price appreciation driven by relative affordability and tight supply
- Inventory recovering but still well below pre-pandemic norms
- Single-family segment drives both price growth and sales volume
- Attached/condo segments showing mixed signals
- Every Missouri county in STL metro falls below national median home price of $430,848
